_comment: Pipeline schema for LLM-extracted structured fields. Promoted from experiments/taxonomy_v3_proposed.json after a 5000-card validation round. See experiments/ for iteration history.

provenance_method._note: Single-select. v2 'mixed' hit 49/500 (10%) — too high — because the model used it as an escape hatch. v3 tightens the description: 'mixed' requires BOTH halves to be load-bearing, not just 'I'm a bit unsure.'

categories.name: human-created
categories.description: Primary-source content written, recorded, or annotated by humans. Includes crowdsourced labels, expert annotations, human-written prompts/responses, and speaker recordings where humans are the source of the content.

categories.name: web-scraped
categories.description: Harvested from public web sources (websites, social platforms, APIs). The distinguishing feature is that a crawler/scraper collected content that already existed on the web, without re-authoring it.

categories.name: sensor-recorded
categories.description: Captured from physical instruments or environments: robot trajectories, sensor streams, microphone recordings of natural sound, camera captures of real-world phenomena. Distinct from human-created (which implies humans as the content source) and web-scraped (which implies pre-existing web data).

categories.name: llm-generated
categories.description: Content produced by an LLM or generative model, including synthetic instructions, synthetic preferences, distilled outputs, and model-written explanations. Include cases where a model generated the data even if humans later filtered it.

categories.name: translated
categories.description: Primarily a translation of another dataset from one language to another. The content structure carries over; only the language changes. Include both machine-translated (DeepL, GPT-4) and human-translated datasets.

categories.name: filtered-subset
categories.description: A selected, cleaned, or otherwise filtered subset of a single parent dataset. The content is unchanged; the curation is in what was kept or dropped.

categories.name: remix
categories.description: Combined from multiple existing datasets. Typical of SFT mixtures and preference mixtures that aggregate several upstream corpora.

categories.name: algorithmically-derived
categories.description: Computed from another dataset by a deterministic or algorithmic process (embeddings, tokenizations, automatic annotations via classical methods, rendered variations). Not primarily LLM-generated, not primarily a filter.

categories.name: mixed
categories.description: USE RARELY. Only choose this when the card explicitly describes TWO OR MORE load-bearing creation methods that jointly produce the data (e.g. 'human-written prompts with LLM-generated responses'). Do NOT use 'mixed' as a fallback for uncertainty — if one method dominates, choose that method even if others were involved. Do NOT use 'mixed' merely because the data was later filtered or translated — filtered-subset and translated are their own categories.

categories.name: other
categories.description: Card describes a method that doesn't fit any category above. Also use for unusual cases like procedurally-generated game data.

categories.name: not_stated
categories.description: The card does not describe how the data was created.

subject_domain._note: v2 had 19 slugs + 4 cards that wanted 'multi-domain' (invalid in v2). v3 adds 'multi-domain' as a legitimate slug for benchmarks/corpora that span multiple subject areas by design.

categories.name: general-web-text
categories.description: Open-domain web crawl, Wikipedia-style corpora, general-purpose text for pretraining. Not topically focused.

categories.name: instruction-and-chat
categories.description: Instruction-following data, assistant dialogues, preference pairs for chat alignment, roleplay. The content's PURPOSE is chat/instruction training, regardless of underlying topic.

categories.name: code-and-software
categories.description: Source code, code completions, code review, developer documentation, software engineering tasks.

categories.name: math-and-reasoning
categories.description: Math problems, formal reasoning, theorem proving, logical puzzles, chain-of-thought reasoning traces.

categories.name: scientific-research
categories.description: Academic papers, research abstracts, chemistry, physics, molecular/materials data, scientific literature.

categories.name: medical-and-biomedical
categories.description: Medical imaging, clinical notes, biomedical text, patient data, health records, pharmacology.

categories.name: natural-images-and-video
categories.description: Photographs, natural video, real-world visual content. Image classification, object detection, scene understanding datasets with non-synthetic sources.

categories.name: generated-media
categories.description: AI-generated images, synthetic video, AI-generated audio. Datasets whose content IS synthetic media (distinct from datasets ABOUT detection of synthetic media).

categories.name: speech-and-audio
categories.description: Speech recordings, TTS training data, ASR corpora, music, environmental audio, audio classification.

categories.name: legal-and-policy
categories.description: Legal documents, case law, regulations, government policy, terms of service, compliance text.

categories.name: finance-and-business
categories.description: Financial reports, market data, trading, cryptocurrency, e-commerce records, business documents, real-estate listings.

categories.name: safety-alignment
categories.description: Safety training data, red-team prompts, jailbreak attempts, toxicity classification, adversarial evaluation.

categories.name: robotics-and-embodied
categories.description: Robot trajectories, manipulation data, embodied AI, humanoid control, sim-to-real.

categories.name: 3d-and-simulation
categories.description: 3D models, meshes, point clouds, physics simulations, synthetic environments.

categories.name: agent-and-tool-use
categories.description: Agentic trajectories, tool-calling demonstrations, multi-step task execution, function-calling.

categories.name: entertainment-and-gaming
categories.description: Video game data, sports, movies/TV, fiction, creative content consumption.

categories.name: geospatial
categories.description: Maps, satellite imagery, geographic data, remote sensing, GIS.

categories.name: multi-domain
categories.description: Benchmark or corpus that deliberately spans multiple subject areas (e.g. MMLU covers 57 subjects; multi-task eval suites; general-knowledge question banks). Use when multi-domain IS the design, not just when the card is ambiguous.

categories.name: other
categories.description: Subject matter that doesn't fit any category above. Prefer a specific category when possible.

categories.name: not_stated
categories.description: The card doesn't describe what the data is about in enough detail to categorize.

training_stage._note: Unchanged from v2. Multi-select because dual-use cards are real.

categories.name: pretraining
categories.description: Large unlabeled or weakly-labeled corpus for pretraining a base model.

categories.name: sft
categories.description: Supervised fine-tuning data: instruction-response pairs or task-specific labeled examples.

categories.name: preference
categories.description: Preference pairs, DPO/RLHF training data, reward modeling data.

categories.name: eval
categories.description: Benchmark or evaluation dataset. Intended for measuring models, not training them.

categories.name: domain-finetune
categories.description: Fine-tuning data for domain adaptation (legal LLM, medical LLM, code LLM). Distinct from general SFT.

categories.name: raw-corpus
categories.description: Raw collected data (scrape, recordings) not yet packaged for a specific training stage. Downstream users decide what to do with it.

categories.name: other
categories.description: Some other training-adjacent use (e.g. reward-model training data that's distinct from preference pairs).

categories.name: not_stated
categories.description: The card doesn't describe intended training use.

format_convention._note: NEW in v3. Captures de-facto community dataset-format conventions (ShareGPT, Alpaca, DPO pairs, VQA, etc.) — a structural/schema property orthogonal to subject, stage, and provenance. Motivated by Toponymy labels like 'Role-Playing ShareGPT Datasets' (107) and 'Alpaca Instruction Tuning' (62) that existing fields couldn't distinguish.

categories.name: sharegpt
categories.description: ShareGPT-style conversation format: list of turns with {from: 'human'|'gpt', value: text}. Used by many roleplay and chat-SFT datasets.

categories.name: alpaca
categories.description: Alpaca-style triples: {instruction, input, output}. Typical of early instruction-tuning datasets.

categories.name: preference-pairs
categories.description: Preference data with chosen/rejected responses per prompt. Covers DPO, RLHF-style, and reward-modeling pair formats.

categories.name: prompt-completion
categories.description: Single-turn prompt/completion or input/output pairs that are NOT Alpaca-style (no separate instruction field) and NOT multi-turn chat.

categories.name: vqa
categories.description: Visual question answering: image + question + answer (including document VQA, chart VQA, video VQA).

categories.name: image-caption
categories.description: Image + caption pair datasets (no question). Used for T2I training and image-text alignment.

categories.name: audio-transcript
categories.description: Audio clip paired with text transcript or caption. Covers ASR training, TTS training, and audio captioning.

categories.name: raw-text
categories.description: Unstructured text documents or passages (pretraining corpora, web scrapes). No per-example labels or turn structure.

categories.name: tabular
categories.description: Row-column structured records (CSV/parquet with multiple feature columns per row). Typical of domain records like real-estate listings or financial data.

categories.name: multi-turn-chat
categories.description: Multi-turn conversational data that does NOT use the ShareGPT field convention (e.g. OpenAI chat format with role/content, or custom JSON).

categories.name: other
categories.description: A clearly-described format that doesn't match any category above.

categories.name: not_stated
categories.description: The card doesn't describe the data format clearly enough to classify.

special_characteristics._note: NEW in v3. Multi-select LIST — most datasets will return [] (empty list). Captures orthogonal dataset-level properties that aren't tied to domain, stage, or provenance. Motivated by Toponymy labels like 'Long Context LLM Evaluation' (74), 'Synthetic Reasoning Datasets' (66), and 'Role-Playing Character Datasets' (60).
special_characteristics.rule: Return a LIST of zero or more applicable slugs. MOST DATASETS SHOULD RETURN []. Only add a slug when the card makes the property explicit and central to the dataset's purpose. Never use slugs outside this list; if nothing applies, return [].

categories.name: long-context
categories.description: Examples deliberately extend to long input lengths (typically >4K tokens, often much longer). The long-context nature is explicit.

categories.name: multi-turn
categories.description: Dialogue data with 3+ back-and-forth turns per example (beyond the standard single user-assistant exchange).

categories.name: adversarial
categories.description: Designed to be hard or to probe failure modes: red-team prompts, jailbreak attempts, adversarial examples, trick questions.

categories.name: reasoning-traces
categories.description: Includes step-by-step reasoning, chain-of-thought, scratchpad content, or explanation of intermediate steps alongside final answers.

categories.name: low-resource-language
categories.description: Focus on underrepresented or low-resource languages where data is scarce. (Don't tag this for widely-resourced languages like English/Chinese/Spanish.)

categories.name: multilingual-parallel
categories.description: Parallel translations of the same content across multiple languages (translation corpora, parallel QA benchmarks).

categories.name: roleplay
categories.description: Character roleplay, persona-based dialogue, or fictional-scenario chat data.

categories.name: long-form-generation
categories.description: Target outputs are long-form (essays, articles, stories, reports) rather than short answers or single sentences.

geo_scope._note: Unchanged from v2. Policy: force not_stated unless explicit.
geo_scope.rule: Return a list of country/region names if the card is explicit. Return ['global'] ONLY if the card explicitly says the content is worldwide/international in scope. Return ['not_applicable'] for content that has no meaningful geography (math, code, generic text, generated media, abstract tasks). Return ['not_stated'] if the card is silent — this is the default, not 'global'. ALWAYS return a JSON list, even for single values.

upstream_models.rule: Return a list of model names explicitly mentioned in the card as having generated or been distilled into this data (e.g. 'GPT-4', 'Claude 3.5 Sonnet', 'Llama-3.1-70B'). Return ['not_applicable'] if the card doesn't involve model-generated content. Return ['not_stated'] if the card describes model generation but doesn't name the models. ALWAYS return a JSON list, even for single values.

is_benchmark.rule: true if the dataset is explicitly intended as an evaluation benchmark (held-out test, leaderboard, eval suite). false for training corpora even if they could be used for eval.
